meta.title: Scout Console | Troop 553B
meta.subtitle: The Complete Scout Management System
meta.description: Four Systems — One Scout — One Hub

badges.name: First Aid
badges.desc: Learn to respond to emergencies and provide first aid care.

badges.name: Citizenship in Community
badges.desc: Explore how local government works and serve your community.

badges.name: Citizenship in Nation
badges.desc: Understand the American political system and citizen responsibilities.

badges.name: Citizenship in World
badges.desc: Learn about world cultures, economies, and your global role.

badges.name: Communication
badges.desc: Develop writing, speaking, and listening skills.

badges.name: Cooking
badges.desc: Master outdoor and home cooking skills including nutrition planning.

badges.name: Personal Fitness
badges.desc: Create and follow a 12-week personal fitness program.

badges.name: Emergency Preparedness
badges.desc: Prepare for emergencies and develop a family emergency plan.

badges.name: Environmental Science
badges.desc: Study ecology, pollution, endangered species, and conservation.

badges.name: Swimming
badges.desc: Demonstrate swimming strokes, endurance, and water safety.

badges.name: Camping
badges.desc: Develop camping skills over 20 nights in the outdoors.

badges.name: Family Life
badges.desc: Strengthen family relationships and understand responsibilities.

badges.name: Personal Management
badges.desc: Learn budgeting, career exploration, and time management.

badges.name: Wilderness Survival
badges.desc: Build shelters, find water, and survive in the backcountry.

badges.name: Orienteering
badges.desc: Navigate using map and compass across unfamiliar terrain.

badges.name: Geocaching
badges.desc: Use GPS technology for outdoor treasure hunting adventures.

badges.name: Hiking
badges.desc: Plan and complete hikes of increasing distance and difficulty.

badges.name: Backpacking
badges.desc: Multi-day backcountry travel with everything on your back.

badges.name: Astronomy
badges.desc: Identify constellations, planets, and understand the cosmos.

badges.name: Bird Study
badges.desc: Identify birds by sight and sound across various habitats.

badges.name: Fishing
badges.desc: Learn fishing techniques, tackle, and fish identification.

badges.name: Weather
badges.desc: Predict weather patterns, understand climate, and track storms.

badges.name: Citizenship in Society
badges.desc: Explore diversity, equity, inclusion, and ethical leadership.

badges.name: Lifesaving
badges.desc: Learn rescue techniques for water emergencies.

badges.name: Public Health
badges.desc: Study disease prevention, sanitation, and community health.

badges.name: Robotics
badges.desc: Design, build, and program a working robot.

badges.name: Digital Technology
badges.desc: Understand digital tools, online safety, and technology impact.

badges.name: Programming
badges.desc: Write programs to solve problems and automate tasks.

badges.name: Art
badges.desc: Explore drawing, painting, sculpture, and visual composition.

badges.name: Photography
badges.desc: Master camera techniques, composition, and storytelling.

badges.name: Woodwork
badges.desc: Build projects safely using hand tools and power tools.

badges.name: Archery
badges.desc: Learn proper archery technique, safety, and scoring.

badges.name: Climbing
badges.desc: Rock climbing, rappelling, belaying, and rope safety.

badges.name: Cycling
badges.desc: Bike maintenance, road safety, and distance riding skills.

ranks.name: Scout
ranks.summary: Learn the fundamentals of Scouting — the Oath, Law, motto, and basic outdoor readiness.
requirements: Repeat the Scout Oath, Scout Law, motto, and slogan from memory
Demonstrate the Scout sign, salute, and handshake
Demonstrate tying the square knot
Understand and commit to the Scout Oath and Law
Describe the Scout uniform and when to wear it
Repeat the Outdoor Code from memory
ranks.notes: Scoutmaster Conference required. No Board of Review at this rank.

ranks.name: Tenderfoot
ranks.summary: Build foundational skills in first aid, fitness, camping, and Scout spirit.
requirements: Participate in 10 separate troop/patrol activities since joining
Demonstrate first aid for simple cuts, scratches, blisters, and nosebleeds
Complete a 1-mile hike and discuss trail safety
Demonstrate basic campsite selection and tent setup
Record two separate campfire or stove meals
Complete the BSA swim classification or Safety Afloat training
Demonstrate Scout spirit for at least 2 months
Complete a Scoutmaster conference
ranks.notes: Board of Review required. Minimum 2 months active participation.

ranks.name: Second Class
ranks.summary: Develop outdoor cooking, navigation, nature awareness, and community citizenship.
requirements: Using a compass, complete an orienteering course
Identify and cook a trail meal using a lightweight stove
Demonstrate first aid for heat/cold emergencies and sprains
Identify 10 native plants and 5 wildlife species in your area
Participate in a flag ceremony and explain flag etiquette
Complete 2 hours of community service
Demonstrate Scout spirit for at least 2 months since Tenderfoot
Complete a Scoutmaster conference
ranks.notes: Board of Review required. Must have Tenderfoot rank first.

ranks.name: First Class
ranks.summary: Demonstrate advanced outdoor skills, leadership, and full wilderness preparedness.
requirements: Plan and lead a 5-mile hike using map and compass
Demonstrate advanced first aid: CPR, splinting, shock treatment
Cook two meals over open fire using raw ingredients
Lash together a camp gadget from natural materials
Swim 75 yards using two different strokes
Identify constellations and use stars for basic navigation
Perform 4 hours of community service
Demonstrate Scout spirit for at least 2 months since Second Class
Complete a Scoutmaster conference
ranks.notes: Board of Review required. Opens the door to Star rank and merit badge focus.

ranks.name: Star
ranks.summary: Earn merit badges, serve your community, and take on a leadership role in the troop.
requirements: Be active in troop for at least 4 months since First Class
Earn 6 merit badges (at least 4 must be Eagle-required)
Serve actively in a troop leadership position for 4 months
Complete at least 6 hours of community service
Participate in a Scoutmaster conference
Complete a Board of Review
ranks.notes: First rank where merit badges are specifically counted. Leadership service begins.

ranks.name: Life
ranks.summary: Deepen merit badge collection, expand leadership, and increase service commitment.
requirements: Be active in troop for at least 6 months since Star
Earn 11 merit badges total (at least 7 must be Eagle-required)
Serve actively in a troop leadership position for 6 months
Complete at least 6 hours of community service
Participate in a Scoutmaster conference
Complete a Board of Review
ranks.notes: Last rank before Eagle. Eagle project planning typically begins here.

ranks.name: Eagle
ranks.summary: Demonstrate exceptional leadership, service, and mastery across all areas of Scouting.
requirements: Be active in troop for at least 6 months since Life
Earn 21 merit badges total (all 13 Eagle-required included)
Serve actively in a troop leadership position for 6 months
Plan, develop, and lead an Eagle Scout service project
Complete an Eagle Scout application
Participate in a Scoutmaster conference
Successfully complete an Eagle Board of Review
ranks.notes: Eagle Scout is the highest rank. Only about 4% of Scouts earn this rank. The Eagle service project must benefit the community and demonstrate leadership.

sections.name: Rank Requirements
sections.desc: Track progress toward each rank with interactive checklists.

sections.name: Merit Badge Tracker
sections.desc: Log started, in-progress, and completed merit badges.

sections.name: Service Hours
sections.desc: Record community service hours with dates and descriptions.

sections.name: Camping Log
sections.desc: Track camping nights, locations, and outdoor experiences.

sections.name: Leadership Positions
sections.desc: Document leadership roles served and duration.

sections.name: Skills Sign-Offs
sections.desc: Digital record of completed skill demonstrations.

systems.name: Rank Track
systems.desc: Scout through Eagle — the backbone of advancement

systems.name: Merit Badges
systems.desc: Eagle-required and elective skills with counselors

systems.name: Camping
systems.desc: Monthly trips, summer camp, and outdoor logging

systems.name: Uniform
systems.desc: Rank patches, sash, position and event patches

terms.def: A three-part document used to track progress on a specific merit badge. It must be signed by the Scoutmaster before starting and by the counselor upon completion.
The adult leader responsible for the program of the troop. They work with the SPL to ensure the troop is scout-led.
The top-ranking scout leader in the troop, elected by members. They lead the PLC and oversee all troop operations.
The committee of scout leaders that plans the troop program.
A list of the raw materials, sub-assemblies, intermediate assemblies, sub-components, parts, and the quantities of each needed to manufacture an end product.
A formal meeting between a Scout and the Scoutmaster (or designee) to discuss progress and readiness for the next rank.
